schedule.name: Daytona 500
schedule.summary: Tyler Reddick made history, winning the 68th Daytona 500 from the front. The 23XI Racing driver led 89 of 200 laps in dominant fashion, beginning what would become a record-setting start to the season.

story_cards.title: Tyler Reddick wins Daytona 500
story_cards.body: Tyler Reddick made history, winning the 68th Daytona 500 from the front. The 23XI Racing driver led 89 of 200 laps in dominant fashion, beginning what would become a record-setting start to the season.

story_cards.title: Bowman's career-best
story_cards.body: Alex Bowman delivered his best Daytona 500 result with a 2nd place finish, staying clean all day in a race that claimed several top contenders.

schedule.name: Autotrader 400
schedule.summary: Reddick became the sixth driver in history to win the first two races of a Cup season. EchoPark's superspeedway-style pack racing played to his strengths at 23XI Racing's home turf.

story_cards.title: Tyler Reddick wins Autotrader 400
story_cards.body: Reddick became the sixth driver in history to win the first two races of a Cup season. EchoPark's superspeedway-style pack racing played to his strengths at 23XI Racing's home turf.

story_cards.title: William Byron's strong showing
story_cards.body: Byron led 45 laps and finished 3rd, suggesting Hendrick Motorsports would be a force to reckon with on intermediate tracks throughout 2026.

schedule.name: DuraMax Texas Grand Prix
schedule.summary: Reddick became the first driver in NASCAR history to win the first three races of a season. He led 58 of 95 laps and held off road-course specialist Shane van Gisbergen in the closing laps. Ross Chastain won Stage 1, Ty Gibbs won Stage 2.

story_cards.title: Tyler Reddick wins DuraMax Texas Grand Prix
story_cards.body: Reddick became the first driver in NASCAR history to win the first three races of a season. He led 58 of 95 laps and held off road-course specialist Shane van Gisbergen in the closing laps. Ross Chastain won Stage 1, Ty Gibbs won Stage 2.

story_cards.title: Herbst's road course ability
story_cards.body: Riley Herbst finished 4th at COTA, showcasing why the Stewart-Haas/HMX car was signed. A genuine surprise performance against veteran road racers.

schedule.name: Straight Talk Wireless 500
schedule.summary: Ryan Blaney ended Reddick's run, taking the lead with 28 laps to go and holding on. Christopher Bell led 176 laps from pole but a late call cost him the win.

story_cards.title: Ryan Blaney wins Straight Talk Wireless 500
story_cards.body: Ryan Blaney ended Reddick's run, taking the lead with 28 laps to go and holding on. Christopher Bell led 176 laps from pole but a late call cost him the win.

story_cards.title: 750-hp package debuts on oval
story_cards.body: Phoenix saw the new 750-horsepower, lower-downforce package debut on an oval. Drivers praised the improved side-by-side racing it produced in the final stage.

schedule.name: Pennzoil 400 Presented by Jiffy Lube
schedule.summary: Hamlin overcame a pit road speeding penalty to lead 134 laps and claim his 61st career win — 10th on NASCAR's all-time list. Christopher Bell won Stage 1, William Byron won Stage 2. It was an emotional win after Hamlin's 2025 Championship heartbreak.

story_cards.title: Denny Hamlin wins Pennzoil 400 Presented by Jiffy Lube
story_cards.body: Hamlin overcame a pit road speeding penalty to lead 134 laps and claim his 61st career win — 10th on NASCAR's all-time list. Christopher Bell won Stage 1, William Byron won Stage 2. It was an emotional win after Hamlin's 2025 Championship heartbreak.

story_cards.title: Toyota dominance
story_cards.body: Toyota swept the top three qualifying spots and delivered four cars in the top seven. JGR's intermediate track package is clearly the one to beat on 1.5-mile ovals in 2026.

story_cards.title: Briscoe bounces back
story_cards.body: Chase Briscoe overcame a pit road speeding penalty to finish 8th — his second top-10 of 2026. A vital result for the Joe Gibbs Racing newcomer after early struggles.

schedule.name: Goodyear 400
schedule.summary: Reddick's fourth win in six starts — the most dominant start to a Cup season in the modern era. He converted pole to win at The Lady in Black, leading 77 laps including the final 28. Brad Keselowski swept both stages and led the most laps but had to settle for second.

story_cards.title: Tyler Reddick wins Goodyear 400
story_cards.body: Reddick's fourth win in six starts — the most dominant start to a Cup season in the modern era. He converted pole to win at The Lady in Black, leading 77 laps including the final 28. Brad Keselowski swept both stages and led the most laps but had to settle for second.

story_cards.title: Keselowski's heartbreak
story_cards.body: Brad Keselowski led 142 of 293 laps — more than anyone — swept both stages, and still finished second. The RFK Racing driver is winning races on pace but struggling to convert.

story_cards.title: Cindric's breakthrough
story_cards.body: Austin Cindric finished 5th at Darlington, his best result of the 2026 season. Penske's No.2 car looked strong all weekend on the egg-shaped oval.

schedule.name: Cook Out 400
schedule.preview: Pole: Denny Hamlin (98.241 mph). P2: William Byron. Race goes green at 3:30 PM ET on FS1. Hamlin is defending winner and 6-time Martinsville champion. Reddick starts P8 looking for a 5th win.

schedule.name: NASCAR Cup Race at New Hampshire
